name: cvp
documentation:
# cvp

[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/cvp?style=flat-square)](https://pypi.org/project/cvp/)
![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/cvp?style=flat-square)

Computer Vision Player

## Features

* Graph based visual programming
* ONVIF Client
* WsDiscovery
* FFmpeg media player
* Layout management
* Subprocess management
* Window management
* Font browser
* [Planned] Stitching
* [Planned] Labeling
* [Planned] Modeling
* [Planned] Database
* [Planned] CEF

## Developing

```shell
## Create a local Python virtual environment.
./python

## Black formatting
./black.sh

## PEP8 linting
./flake8.sh

## Sort import order
./isort.sh

## Type checking
./mypy.sh

## Unit testing
./pytest.sh
```

## Installation

### from pip

```shell
pip install cvp
```

### from source

```shell
git clone https://github.com/osom8979/cvp "$HOME/.local/share/cvp"
cd "$HOME/.local/share/cvp"
./run
```

## License

See the [LICENSE](./LICENSE) file for details. In summary,
**cvp** is licensed under the **MIT license**.

modules.name: cvp.ffmpeg.flags.cpuflags
documentation:
Allows setting and clearing cpu flags.

This option is intended for testing.

Do not use it unless you know what you’re doing.

ffmpeg -cpuflags -sse+mmx ...
ffmpeg -cpuflags mmx ...
ffmpeg -cpuflags 0 ...

https://www.ffmpeg.org/ffmpeg.html#Generic-options

classes.name: cvp.ffmpeg.h264.crf.Crf

class_variables.name: cvp.ffmpeg.h264.crf.Crf.lossless
documentation: for 8 bit only, for 10 bit use -qp 0

Constant Rate Factor

Choose a CRF value:
    The range of the CRF scale is 0–51, where 0 is lossless (for 8 bit only, for 10
    bit use -qp 0), 23 is the default, and 51 is worst quality possible. A lower
    value generally leads to higher quality, and a subjectively sane range is 17–28.
    Consider 17 or 18 to be visually lossless or nearly so; it should look the same
    or nearly the same as the input but it isn't technically lossless.

    The range is exponential, so increasing the CRF value +6 results in roughly half
    the bitrate / file size, while -6 leads to roughly twice the bitrate.

    Choose the highest CRF value that still provides an acceptable quality. If the
    output looks good, then try a higher value. If it looks bad, choose a lower
    value.

References:
    https://trac.ffmpeg.org/wiki/Encode/H.264#crf

class_variables.name: cvp.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.stitcher_mode_index
documentation:
Scenario for stitcher operation.

PANORAMA
    Mode for creating photo panoramas. Expects images under perspective
    transformation and projects resulting pano to sphere.
    - `cv2.detail.BestOf2NearestMatcher`
    - `cv2.SphericalWarper`
SCANS
    Mode for composing scans. Expects images under affine transformation does
    not compensate exposure by default.
    - `cv2.detail.AffineBestOf2NearestMatcher`
    - `cv2.AffineWarper`

class_variables.name: cvp.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.features_finder_index
documentation: Type of features used for images matching.

class_variables.name: cvp.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.matcher_index
documentation: Matcher used for pairwise image matching.

class_variables.name: cvp.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.estimator_index
documentation: Type of estimator used for transformation estimation.

class_variables.name: cvp.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.match_conf
documentation:
Confidence for feature matching step.

The recommended default values are:
- ORB is 0.3
- other feature types is 0.65

class_variables.name: cvp.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.conf_thresh
documentation: Threshold for two images are from the same panorama confidence.

class_variables.name: cvp.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.ba_refine_mask
documentation:
Set refinement mask for bundle adjustment.

It looks like 'x_xxx', where 'x' means refine respective parameter and '_' means
don't refine, and has the following format:<fx><skew><ppx><aspect><ppy>. The default
mask is 'xxxxx'. If bundle adjustment doesn't support estimation of selected
parameter then the respective flag is ignored.

class_variables.name: cvp.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.compose_mega_pixel
documentation: Resolution for compositing step. Use -1 for original resolution.

class_variables.name: cvp.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.expos_comp_nr_filtering
documentation: Number of filtering iterations of the exposure compensation gains.

class_variables.name: cvp.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.expos_comp_block_size
documentation: BLock size in pixels used by the exposure compensator.

class_variables.name: cvp.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.range_width
documentation: Uses range_width to limit number of images to match with.

class_variables.name: cvp.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.use_cuda
documentation: Try to use CUDA. The default value is no. All default values are for CPU mode.

variables.name: cvp.chrono.filename.DATENAME_SHORT_FORMAT
documentation:
"%H'
    Hour (24-hour clock) as a zero-padded decimal number.
    e.g. 00, 01, …, 23
'%M'
    Minute as a zero-padded decimal number.
    e.g. 00, 01, …, 59
'%S'
    Second as a zero-padded decimal number.
    e.g. 00, 01, …, 59
'%f'
    Microsecond as a decimal number, zero-padded to 6 digits.
    e.g. 000000, 000001, …, 999999

classes.name: cvp.context.autofixer.AutoFixer
documentation: A Retriever class that checks and restores configuration information.

classes.name: cvp.maths.equation.linear.slope_intercept.SlopeInterceptForm

methods.name: cvp.maths.equation.linear.slope_intercept.SlopeInterceptForm.calc_x
documentation:
y = mx + b
y - b = mx
(y - b) / m = x

Equation of a Straight Line

y = mx + b

classes.name: cvp.maths.equation.linear.point_slope.PointSlopeForm

methods.name: cvp.maths.equation.linear.point_slope.PointSlopeForm.calc_y
documentation:
y − y1 = m(x − x1)
y = m(x − x1) + y1

Point-Slope Equation of a Line

y − y1 = m(x − x1)

classes.name: cvp.maths.equation.linear.general.GeneralForm

methods.name: cvp.maths.equation.linear.general.GeneralForm.from_coord_polar
documentation:
m = tan(radians)
y - y1 = m (x - x1)
y = mx - mx1 + y1
0 = mx - mx1 + y1 - y
0 = mx - y + (-mx1 + y1)
A = m
B = -1
C = -m * x1 + y1

methods.name: cvp.maths.equation.linear.general.GeneralForm.calc_x
documentation:
Ax + By + C = 0
Ax = -By -C
Ax = -(By + C)
x = -(By + C) / A

methods.name: cvp.maths.equation.linear.general.GeneralForm.calc_y
documentation:
Ax + By + C = 0
By = -Ax -C
By = -(Ax + C)
y = -(Ax + C) / B

methods.name: cvp.maths.equation.linear.general.GeneralForm.vertical_slope
documentation:
vertical_slope * slope = -1
vertical_slope = -1 / slope
vertical_slope = -1 / (-1 * (self.a / self.b))
vertical_slope = -1 * -1 * (self.b / self.a)
vertical_slope = self.b / self.a

methods.name: cvp.maths.equation.linear.general.GeneralForm.intersection
documentation:
Ax + By + C = 0
Dx + Ey + F = 0
x = (CE - BF) / (BD - AE)
y = (CD - AF) / (AE - BD)

General Form of Equation of a Line

Ax + By + C = 0

variables.name: cvp.variables.DEFAULT_CURVE_TESSELLATION_TOL
documentation:
Tessellation tolerance when using BezierCurve without a specific number of segments.
Decrease for highly tessellated curves (higher quality, more polygons),
Increase to reduce quality.

modules.name: cvp.palette.extended
documentation: Extended colors are the result of merging specifications from HTML 4.01, CSS 2.0,
SVG 1.0 and CSS3 User Interfaces (CSS3 UI).
